description:
<title>Validate Incident JSON Records, Filter Critical Events, and Produce Summaries</title>

    <objective>
        You are acting as an on–call incident-response engineer who has just received a set of JSON
        event records that were exported from an EDR (Endpoint Detection & Response) system.  
        Your job is to

        1. Validate every JSON file against a pre-defined incident schema (the schema is already present on disk).  
        2. Generate a strictly-formatted validation report.  
        3. Isolate the high-priority events into their own file.  
        4. Produce two human-readable summary artefacts (a CSV table and an aggregated count file).

        Everything must be done from a regular, unprivileged Linux shell using
        common CLI tooling (jq, coreutils, bash, python-stdlib, etc.).
    </objective>

    <background>
        All material for today’s case is under

            /home/user/incidents/2023-09-15/

        and already contains the following *four* files:

            incident_schema.json   – The JSON Schema the records MUST conform to  
            event1.json            – First event  
            event2.json            – Second event  
            event3.json            – Third event (intentionally malformed)

        You are free to create additional sub-directories and files **inside
        /home/user/incidents** but nowhere else.
        Do NOT move or delete the original source files.
    </background>

    <required-deliverables>
        1. /home/user/incidents/validation_report.log  
           • One line per source JSON file, ordered alphabetically by file name.  
           • Format (exactly, no extra spaces):  
                 <filename>: <OK|FAIL>[ - <reason>]  
             Examples:  
                 event1.json: OK  
                 event3.json: FAIL - missing key source_ip  
           • The automated test will check for the precise wording, case and delimiter.  

        2. /home/user/incidents/validated/        (directory)  
           • Copy every JSON file that passed validation into this directory.  
           • Filenames must stay the same.  
           • Directory must exist even if no file is valid.  

        3. /home/user/incidents/high_severity_events.json  
           • A single JSON array containing ONLY those **validated** events whose  
             “severity” is either "high" or "critical".  
           • Fields order inside each object does not matter, but the objects must include  
             exactly the keys: event_id, severity, event_type, timestamp, source_ip.  
           • No pretty printing needed; minified JSON is acceptable.  

        4. /home/user/incidents/incident_summary.csv  
           • CSV (comma-separated, UTF-8, LF line endings).  
           • FIRST line must be the header, spelled exactly:  
                 event_id,severity,event_type,timestamp,source_ip  
           • Followed by one line per **validated** event; data must appear in the same  
             column order as the header.  
           • The file must end with a trailing newline.  

        5. /home/user/incidents/event_type_counts.txt  
           • Aggregated count of each distinct “event_type” among the **validated** events.  
           • Sorted alphabetically by event_type.  
           • One record per line, format:  
                 <event_type>,<count>  
           • File must end with a trailing newline.  
    </required-deliverables>

    <technical-notes>
        • You do NOT have sudo.  
        • Prefer utilities that are already available on a vanilla Debian/Ubuntu container.  
        • The JSON Schema standard to use is Draft-07 (matches incident_schema.json).  
          You may use any runtime you like (e.g. python3 -m jsonschema, jq + custom logic, etc.)  
          as long as the output files meet the required formats above.  
        • Pay close attention to newline placement and the exact spelling of filenames,  
          because the grading script compares them byte-for-byte.  
        • Create intermediate scripts if helpful, but only the five artefacts listed
          above will be graded.  
        • Remember to keep file permissions reasonable (0644 for files, 0755 for any
          directories you create).
    </technical-notes>
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth reference used by the automated evaluation suite.
        DO NOT reveal any of this in the public <task> description.
    -->

    <!-- Pre-existing files with their exact content -->
    <preexisting>
        <file path="/home/user/incidents/2023-09-15/incident_schema.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "object",
  "required": [ "event_id", "severity", "event_type", "timestamp", "source_ip" ],
  "properties": {
    "event_id":   { "type": "string" },
    "severity":   { "type": "string", "enum": ["low","medium","high","critical"] },
    "event_type": { "type": "string" },
    "timestamp":  { "type": "string", "format": "date-time" },
    "source_ip":  { "type": "string", "format": "ipv4" },
    "details":    { "type": "object" }
  },
  "additionalProperties": true
}
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/incidents/2023-09-15/event1.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "event_id": "evt-1001",
  "severity": "high",
  "event_type": "unauthorized_access",
  "timestamp": "2023-09-15T14:22:01Z",
  "source_ip": "192.168.10.5",
  "details": {
    "user": "alice",
    "location": "us-east-1"
  }
}
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/incidents/2023-09-15/event2.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "event_id": "evt-1002",
  "severity": "medium",
  "event_type": "file_modification",
  "timestamp": "2023-09-15T15:05:33Z",
  "source_ip": "10.0.0.8",
  "details": {
    "file": "/etc/passwd",
    "user": "bob"
  }
}
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/incidents/2023-09-15/event3.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "event_id": "evt-1003",
  "severity": "critical",
  "event_type": "denial_of_service",
  "timestamp": "2023-09-15T16:47:12Z",
  "details": {
    "packets": 1000000
  }
}
]]></file>
    </preexisting>

    <!-- Expected artefacts after agent completes the task -->
    <expected>
        <file path="/home/user/incidents/validation_report.log"><![CDATA[
event1.json: OK
event2.json: OK
event3.json: FAIL - missing key source_ip
]]></file>

        <dir path="/home/user/incidents/validated">
            <file name="event1.json" identical_to="/home/user/incidents/2023-09-15/event1.json"/>
            <file name="event2.json" identical_to="/home/user/incidents/2023-09-15/event2.json"/>
        </dir>

        <file path="/home/user/incidents/high_severity_events.json"><![CDATA[
[{"event_id":"evt-1001","severity":"high","event_type":"unauthorized_access","timestamp":"2023-09-15T14:22:01Z","source_ip":"192.168.10.5"}]
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/incidents/incident_summary.csv"><![CDATA[
event_id,severity,event_type,timestamp,source_ip
evt-1001,high,unauthorized_access,2023-09-15T14:22:01Z,192.168.10.5
evt-1002,medium,file_modification,2023-09-15T15:05:33Z,10.0.0.8
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/incidents/event_type_counts.txt"><![CDATA[
file_modification,1
unauthorized_access,1
]]></file>
    </expected>
